title: Flea Treatment in Irvington, NJ | $350-$650
metaDescription: Professional flea treatment services in Irvington, NJ starting at $350. Fast, effective flea extermination for homes and businesses. Same-day service available. Call now!
intro: Dealing with a flea infestation in your Irvington home or business? Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive flea treatment services throughout Irvington, NJ, with pricing ranging from $350 to $650 depending on the size and severity of the infestation. Our experienced technicians use proven methods to eliminate fleas at all life stages, ensuring your property is flea-free quickly and safely.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Irvington and the greater New Jersey area for years with reliable, effective pest control solutions. Our licensed technicians understand the unique challenges of flea infestations in Irvington's diverse residential neighborhoods and commercial spaces, delivering customized treatment plans that work the first time.
localContext: Irvington's mix of historic homes, multi-family buildings, and proximity to parks like Olympic Park creates ideal conditions for flea infestations, especially in properties with pets. Whether you're in the tree-lined streets near Irvington High School or in the bustling areas along Springfield Avenue, our team provides prompt, discreet service to protect your family and pets from these persistent pests.

faqs.question: How much does flea treatment cost in Irvington, NJ?
faqs.answer: Flea treatment in Irvington typically ranges from $350 to $650, depending on the size of your property and the extent of the infestation. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any treatment, so you'll know exactly what to expect with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How long does flea treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Most flea treatments begin working immediately upon application, with noticeable reduction in adult fleas within 24 hours. However, complete elimination of all life stages (eggs, larvae, pupae, and adults) typically takes 2-4 weeks. We recommend a follow-up treatment after 14 days to ensure all newly hatched fleas are eliminated.

faqs.question: Is flea treatment safe for my pets and children?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for pets and children when applied according to label instructions. We recommend keeping pets and children away from treated areas for 2-4 hours or until surfaces are completely dry. Our technicians will provide specific safety instructions based on the products used in your home.

faqs.question: Do I need to leave my Irvington home during flea treatment?
faqs.answer: For most flea treatments, you'll need to vacate your home for 2-4 hours while products dry and settle. We'll provide you with detailed preparation instructions, including vacuuming carpets, washing pet bedding, and securing food items. Our team works efficiently to minimize disruption to your daily routine.
